stem: A manufacturing study measures defect rate across 4 factorys, each containing multiple production lines. factory A has 20 observations with mean 32.3 % (within-factory SD = 9.4). factory B has 15 observations with mean 56.6 % (within-factory SD = 7.8). factory C has 5 observations with mean 54.1 % (within-factory SD = 9.4). factory D has 10 observations with mean 40.1 % (within-factory SD = 7.2). The between-factory standard deviation is uncertain, estimated between 6.4 and 15.9 %. Using a hierarchical Bayesian model, estimate the true defect rate for factory D, accounting for shrinkage toward the grand mean and the uncertainty in the between-factory variation.

ground_truth.answer: Hierarchical estimate for factory D: 40.2 % (shrunk from raw 40.1). Range [40.2, 40.5] as between-factory SD varies from 6.4 to 15.9. Pooled mean: 43.3.

solution_steps: Compute pooled (grand) mean: 43.3 % (weighted average of 4 factory means).
Shrinkage factor for factory D: B = σ²_between / (σ²_between + σ²_within/10). At σ_between=10.6: B = 0.956.
Interval [40.2, 40.5] captures uncertainty from the unknown between-group variance.
common_failure_modes: Using the raw observed mean (40.1) without shrinkage
Using only the grand mean (43.3) and ignoring group-specific data
Not recognizing that small-sample groups should be shrunk more toward the grand mean
Treating the between-group variance as known rather than uncertain
